evidence: "A source of joy and entertainment! Poirot is a full-rounded character who carries each case from inception to fruition with 'grace' and ease."
"Found the storyline to be complicated and so was able to go for a coffee break a couple of times."
"The story bears the largest responsibility in The Sea of Trees."
"It is not necessary for a movie to have an intelligent 'story'!"
"The story stayed believable (as opposed to other stories about Mars where preachiness can get in the way)."
"A film's story is always the final arbiter for a successful film but acting, directing and production values must provide equal measure for that overall success to transcend."
"Writing, acting, directing and production are first class and set a very high standard indeed!"
"Love Naomi Watts' film work. She provides a lot more than simple female lead creds she carries her films."
"I have not written many film reviews over the past year or so because, honestly, I have not been moved by the majority for the films I have watched during that timeframe."
"I make it a habit to re-watch the 'Unforgiven' around once a year for the purpose of rekindling my ability to 'smell' the rain."
"The audience is provided with the rationale and the necessary back-story of his wife's personality and her diminishment as she becomes ill and eventually passes away."
"I will remember and cherish this story and this film for the rest of my life."
"The film's production values leap to the center stage with so much pain and sorrow offset by a simple, beautiful, orchid flower that returns late in the story."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for drama and action films, valuing high production quality and emotional resonance. They appreciate complex narratives but have mixed feelings about pacing, indicating a nuanced taste in storytelling.
